new_classes.definition_en: Natural mineral water or artificially carbonated water, being water to which carbon dioxide has been added, as described in HS heading 2201 Notes B and C; excludes natural mineral water put up in aerosol containers for skin care (heading 3304).
A quality borne by a water or beverage indicating the presence of dissolved or added carbon dioxide, distinguishing carbonated (sparkling) water from still water within heading 2201.

A packaging quality or presentation form in which a product (e.g. natural mineral water) is put up in a spray/aerosol container intended for skin-care use, resulting in classification under heading 3304 rather than 2201.

coverage_explanation: The axioms cover the two main positive criteria (mineral water / carbonated water bearing a carbonation quality) and the key exclusion (natural mineral water in aerosol spray cans for skin care → heading 3304). However, the legal text references HS Explanatory Notes B and C for heading 2201 in full, which contain detailed sub-criteria (e.g. specific ion concentration thresholds for recognised natural mineral water, artificial carbonation levels) that cannot be fully formalised without additional data properties or value constraints not yet present in the TBox. The distinction between CN sub-codes 22011011–22011090 (e.g. natural vs. artificially carbonated) is also only partially captured at this level, reducing the coverage score.
